caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: AAPOR describes survey research broadly, not task shares inside US NAICS 541720 lower-middle-market firms.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied occupation mix is five-digit 54172 and the large residual combines unobserved field, research, data and program roles.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Adoption speed, federal funding shocks, pricing and terminal demand are not used to inflate these task fractions.
inputs.pi_dist: FAR describes an available federal contract type, not the NAICS 541720 revenue-weighted contract mix.
inputs.pi_dist: The fixed-price CRM share versus sponsored research share is not observed.
inputs.pi_moat: NPS qualifications and ACRA membership describe the CRM subsegment, not a code-wide switching-cost measurement.
inputs.pi_moat: Credentialed physical fieldwork supports terminal demand separately and is not counted as a second capture mechanism.
inputs.t50_years: AAPOR does not report a representative current-adoption rate or diffusion curve for US lower-middle-market 541720 firms.
inputs.t50_years: This is delivery adoption, not client enthusiasm or a count of experimental AI use.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No source in this packet directly measures current adoption for the target industry, geography and size band.
inputs.historical_analogs: The analogy is qualitative and does not independently set the selected t50 or role fractions.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This is not evidence of a sector-wide succession wave.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Individual firms may still have material succession risk.
inputs.active_consolidators: ACRA membership is not evidence of a consolidator count or of acquisition demand.
inputs.active_consolidators: Used only for buyability, not for AI exposure or adoption.
inputs.buy_mult: No code-specific, target-band EBITDA transaction source was found in the fresh pass.
inputs.buy_mult: Owner compensation, nonprofit contamination, contract concentration and seasonal field labor require target-level normalization.
inputs.exit_mult: No code-specific exit multiple or observed multiple expansion supports this selection.
inputs.exit_mult: The assumed expansion may disappear if AI pressure or federal demand shocks reduce durable earnings.
inputs.pricing_structure: FAR is not evidence that a majority of target-firm revenue uses this contract type.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $201,227 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541720); class <5: 1,062 firms, avg revenue/firm $375,004 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $58,688 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 244 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,139,684 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $178,361 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 184 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,368,761 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $370,711 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 196 firms, avg revenue/firm $7,014,362 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,097,748 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 62 firms, avg revenue/firm $35,454,306 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,548,599 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 39 firms, avg revenue/firm $127,242,462 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $19,913,445 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 258

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI exposure is high for research preparation, survey/document production, coding and analysis, but low for statutory field activity, human services and sponsor accountability.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541720 ($6,516,505,720) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541720 ($9,647,627,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: All deterministic mechanics pass and all five registered URLs were freshly accessible. Four score-driving sources support the stated mechanisms or directional claims but remain disclosed proxies rather than direct measurements of NAICS 541720 task shares, adoption, pricing mix, moat, or consolidator activity. The cautious low-confidence kill conclusion remains useful, with all 20 authored caveats carried forward verbatim and no validator-authored material finding.

sources.title: FAR Subpart 16.3 — Cost-Reimbursement Contracts
sources.what_it_supports: Federal cost-reimbursement contracts pay allowable incurred costs and are used where requirements or costs cannot be estimated accurately enough for fixed price.

sources.title: AAPOR Releases New Report from Task Force on Responsible AI Integration in Survey Research
sources.what_it_supports: AI is being used across questionnaire design, interviewing, data processing, analysis and reporting in survey research, with adoption accelerating.

sources.title: Review of U.S. Department of Education Changes in Staffing and Operations
sources.what_it_supports: Department of Education actions in early 2025 included 129 terminated contracts totaling $1.3 billion and terminated contracts related to educational studies.

sources.title: National Park Service Professional Qualifications Standards
sources.what_it_supports: Archaeological professional qualifications require a graduate degree plus field and professional experience, supporting the physical and credentialed character of the CRM subsegment.

sources.title: American Cultural Resources Association Mission
sources.what_it_supports: ACRA is a national trade association for cultural-resource-management firms of different sizes, types and specialties.

title: Research and Development in the Social Sciences and Humanities
